messages.content:
You are an expert software engineer that generates concise summaries of code-related text.

Reply only with the summary, without any additional text, explanations, or line breaks.

Give a detailed but concise summary of this task.
A third party should be able to understand what happened without reference to the original.
Focus on information that would be useful for someone doing further work on the project described in the task.

Here is the task to summarize. Do not include XML tags or other markup.
<task sequence=4>
  <message type=user>
    **Task**: Review `README.md` and ensure the “What this project is for” section succinctly states: (1) it’s a Python Textual TUI client, (2) it manages a local Java executor subprocess, (3) it authenticates with an HTTP bearer token, and (4) it streams events/tokens to power chat/context/tasks panels. If any of these points are missing or unclear, adjust wording (no behavioral changes).
    
    **Acceptance**: `README.md` contains a clear 2–4 sentence project-purpose description covering the four points above.
    
    **Touch points**: `README.md`
    
    **Key Locations:**
    `README.md` (top section: “What this project is for”)
    
    **Key Discoveries:**
    `README.md` already includes the project’s purpose and high-level architecture (Textual TUI + Java executor subprocess + bearer token auth + streaming updates).
  </message>
  
  <message type=ai>
    [HARNESS NOTE: this is a synthetic summary of your explanations of the edits made.
     You made changes to README.md. These changes are reflected in the Workspace contents above.
     Review the current contents carefully before proposing additional changes to fix the build.]
    
    The `README.md` already contains a high-quality description that covers all four required points. I will make a minor adjustment to the wording to ensure "streaming" is explicitly linked to powering the specific panels (chat, context, tasks) as requested.
    
    
    
    The build failure is caused by unsorted imports in `tests/test_smoke.py`, which is being flagged by `ruff`. However, `tests/test_smoke.py` is not currently in the editable workspace.
    
    Regarding the original goal, the description in `README.md` already covers the four required points. I will refine the wording slightly to more explicitly link the streaming events to the specific panels as requested.
    
    
    To fix the build error in `tests/test_smoke.py`, please add that file to the context so I can organize the imports according to the project's standards.
  </message>
  
  <message type=user>
    The build failed.
    Please analyze the error message and provide SEARCH/REPLACE blocks to fix all the errors and warnings in service of the original goal.
    You should use the conversation history to understand your earlier thinking; since your changes
    have been incorporated into the Workspace, look at the current state of those files before
    generate SEARCH/REPLACE blocks. You have already made changes, but you made mistakes;
    that is why the build is failing!
    
    IMPORTANT: If solving the build or failure requires editing files or using APIs you do not have
    in your Workspace, do your best to explain the problem but DO NOT provide any edits.
    Otherwise, provide the edits as usual.
    
    <build_output>
    process 'uv run ruff check .' signaled error code 1
    
    I001 [*] Import block is un-sorted or un-formatted
       --> tests/test_smoke.py:553:5
        |
    551 |   def test_chat_panel_notification_safety():
    552 |       """Verify notification panel handles markup safely."""
    553 | /     from unittest.mock import MagicMock
    554 | |     from rich.text import Text
    555 | |     from brokk_code.widgets.chat_panel import ChatPanel
    556 | |     from textual.widgets import RichLog
        | |_______________________________________^
    557 |
    558 |       panel = ChatPanel()
        |
    help: Organize imports
    
    Found 1 error.
    [*] 1 fixable with the `--fix` option.
    </build_output>
    
    Remember to pay close attention to the SEARCH/REPLACE block format instructions and examples!
  </message>
  
  <message type=ai>
    I will refine the description in `README.md` to ensure all four points are explicitly and succinctly stated as requested.
    
    Regarding the build failure in `tests/test_smoke.py`, I do not have that file in my editable workspace. Please add `tests/test_smoke.py` to the context so I can fix the import sorting error identified by `ruff`.
    
    ```
    README.md
    <<<<<<< SEARCH
    ## What this project is for
    
    This project is a Python (Textual) terminal UI client for Brokk that launches and manages a local Java executor subprocess. It authenticates via an HTTP bearer token to submit jobs and streams real-time events or tokens to power its interactive chat, context, and task panels.
    =======
    ## What this project is for
    
    This project is a Python (Textual) terminal UI client for Brokk that launches and manages a local Java executor subprocess. It authenticates using an HTTP bearer token to submit jobs and streams real-time events and tokens to power its interactive chat, context, and task panels.
    >>>>>>> REPLACE
    ```
  </message>
</task>
